channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Telangana Woman Survives 21-Hour Ordeal After Being Robbed and Pushed Into Well
item.description: A 55-year-old daily wage worker from Telangana's Karimnagar district survived a harrowing 21-hour ordeal after being allegedly robbed and pushed into an agricultural well by a student grappling with ₹...

item.title: Tanzania and Russia Formalize Tourism Partnership with Direct Flights
item.description: Tanzania and Russia have formalized their tourism partnership through a new Memorandum of Understanding, coinciding with the launch of direct flights between the two nations. The agreement aims to dee...

item.title: VHP Requests Ayodhya Police to Investigate Kejriwal and Priyanka Gandhi in Ram Temple Donation Misappropriation Case
item.description: The Vishva Hindu Parishad (VHP), an RSS affiliate, has formally requested the Ayodhya Police to investigate allegations made by Opposition leaders Arvind Kejriwal and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra regarding t...

item.title: Rachel Otuoma Survives High-Speed Crash on Nairobi Expressway
item.description: Rachel Otuoma, a Kenyan TikToker and widow of footballer Ezekiel Otuoma, shared details of a terrifying road accident she survived on the Nairobi Expressway. A speeding vehicle lost control, veered in...

item.title: UK's Nuclear Reactor Ambitions Amid Political Transition
item.description: The article discusses the potential end of Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Small Modular Reactor (SMR) initiative as he prepares to step down. Starmer, who has championed SMRs to power AI data centers, ...

item.title: Government and NGOs collaborate to provide food for migrants at Musina repatriation centre
item.description: The South African government is not directly funding food supplies for foreign nationals awaiting repatriation at the Musina Temporary Repatriation Centre, according to Limpopo Home Affairs manager Al...

item.title: NDLEA arrests Nigerian-British woman with cocaine concealed in fake plantains
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has made significant drug seizures across Nigeria, highlighting its ongoing efforts to combat illicit drug trafficking. A 67-year-old Nigerian-British ...

item.title: England clinch Twenty20 victory over India with Bethell's innings
item.description: In a thrilling Twenty20 encounter, England secured a decisive win against India, with Jacob Bethell's unbeaten 76 leading the charge. Despite the debutant brilliance of 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavansh...

item.title: Nanny and Employer Clash Over Dispute Over Employment Termination
item.description: Kenyan actress Jackie Matubia is at the center of a controversy after her long-time nanny, Brigid Nanjala, broke her silence to address claims that she left her service without notice. The nanny revea...

item.title: Work and Leisure Balances During England’s World Cup Match Against Mexico
item.description: The article explores the challenges employees face when balancing work obligations with attending England's World Cup match against Mexico. With a 1am kick-off potentially extending into the early hou...

item.title: World Cup 2026 Round of 16 Matchups Analyzed
item.description: The article provides an analysis of the eight Round of 16 matchups in the World Cup 2026, ranking them based on potential outcomes and odds. Despite some favorites like Germany, Netherlands, and Croat...

item.title: Harry Styles Honors One Direction Bandmates at Final Wembley Concert
item.description: Harry Styles concluded his record-breaking 12-night residency at Wembley Stadium, becoming the first artist to perform 12 shows at the venue on a single tour. During his final sold-out concert, Styles...

item.title: Queensland Premier Announces New Crime Policy
item.description: Queensland Premier David Crisafulli has announced a comprehensive new crime policy aimed at addressing rising criminal activity across the state. The policy, unveiled on July 5, 2026, includes measure...

item.title: Clinical Trial Shows Fish Oil Supplements Don't Prevent Dementia
item.description: A large-scale clinical trial published by USC School of Medicine found that high-dose DHA supplements, while reaching the brain, did not improve memory or cognitive function in older adults at risk fo...

item.title: Cape Verde Players' Salaries Revealed After Historic World Cup Run
item.description: Cape Verde made waves in the 2026 FIFA World Cup with their remarkable performances, earning global acclaim for their valiant efforts against top-tier teams like Argentina and Spain. The nation's foot...

item.title: Toke Makinwa Discusses Transactional Relationships, Money, and Men in Controversial Comments

item.title: Collingwood Defender Brayden Maynard Fined for Umpire Push Incident
item.description: Collingwood defender Brayden Maynard has been fined $5,000 by the AFL Match Review Officer (MRO) for pushing umpire Nicholas Brown during a heated clash with Gold Coast Suns player Ben Long. The incid...

item.title: Taiwan reinstates military education on China security threats
item.description: Taiwan's defence ministry has restarted 'anti-communist' patriotic education for military academy graduates after a 24-year hiatus, citing heightened risks from China. The decision follows reports of ...

item.title: Ancient Star Cluster Reveals Early Universe Secrets via Hubble Telescope
item.description: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has captured an image of NGC 6426, a globular cluster in the Milky Way's outer halo, celebrating the U.S. 250th anniversary. This 13-billion-year-old cluster offers insig...

item.title: Canada's Football Journey Marks a New Era After World Cup Exit
item.description: Canada's national football team expressed gratitude to fans after their World Cup elimination, marking a transformative moment for the sport in the country. Despite a 3-0 defeat to Morocco in the roun...
